message:
fix(security): add domain separators to SHA-256 hashes and include missing fields in audit chains

Systematic fix for hash collision vulnerabilities across control plane,
security, policy, and supply chain modules. Two classes of issues addressed:

1. Missing domain separators in SHA-256 hash computations:
   Without separators between concatenated fields, distinct inputs can
   produce identical byte streams (e.g., fields "ab"+"cd" and "a"+"bcd"
   hash identically). Adding `|` byte separators between each field
   ensures unique hash inputs for distinct field combinations.

   Affected modules:
   - audience_token: AudienceBoundToken::hash()
   - evidence_replay_gate: CapturedEvidence::compute_input_hash()
   - approval_workflow: compute_entry_hash()
   - challenge_flow: ChallengeAuditEntry::hash()
   - impossible_default: CapabilityToken::content_hash() and
     EnforcementAuditEntry::hash()

   divergence_gate: OperatorAuthorization now uses a canonical format
   string with `|` delimiters instead of separate hasher.update() calls
   with mixed text/binary encodings, making the hash scheme consistent
   and auditable.

2. Missing fields in audit chain hashes:
   - certification: include extension_id in entry hash — previously two
     entries for different extensions with the same sequence/timestamp
     could produce identical hashes
   - quarantine: include severity level in entry hash — quarantine
     decisions at different severity levels were not distinguishable
     in the hash chain
   - reputation: include publisher_id in entry hash — entries for
     different publishers with the same event type could collide

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
